pi_items.source: Confirmed via export_result.sh which explicitly writes to /tmp/rental_asset_result.json and verifier.py which reads from it.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which generates 5 RED Komodos in stock, and the prompt instructions which require moving RED-KOM-002 and RED-KOM-005 to Out on Rent.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which generates RED Komodos 001-005 in stock. With 002 and 005 moved to rent, 001, 003, and 004 remain in stock, matching verifier.py anti-gaming logic.
Confirmed via the prompt instructions in setup_task.sh which request transferring 5x V-Mount Battery 98Wh to Out on Rent, matched by verifier.py.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which injects SD-MIX-012 into Stock and SD-MIX-011 into Rent. Returning SD-MIX-011 to Stock puts both in Stock, fulfilling the prompt instructions.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which creates 10 boom mics in stock and 3 in rent. Returning 2 from rent to stock results in 12, as enforced by verifier.py.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh's prompt instructions to return 1 damaged boom mic to Maintenance, explicitly evaluated in verifier.py.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which creates SNY-VEN-001 in rent, and the prompt which explicitly forbids moving other clients' equipment. Verified by anti-gaming logic in verifier.py.
Confirmed via verifier.py which uses 80 as the default pass_threshold.

privileged_info_summary: The task utilizes synthetically generated inventory data within an Odoo instance, fully seeded via XMLRPC in setup_task.sh. The verified expectations exactly align with the prompt instructions: RED Komodos 002 and 005 and 5 V-Mount batteries are moved to rent; Mixer SD-MIX-011 and 2 Boom Mics return to stock; and 1 Boom Mic goes to maintenance. The unmentioned RED Komodos (001, 003, 004), Mixer SD-MIX-012, and Sony Venice SNY-VEN-001 remain in their initial locations as verified by the anti-gaming constraints.
